positional_bias.note: max_share == 0.40, not strictly greater than threshold; boundary pass. Meta/GIFT positions cross-checked: derived [1,3,0,2,1] matches sidecar — no desync.

length_bias.note: rate == 0.40, not strictly greater than threshold; boundary pass. q5 individual ratio is 1.857 (soft flag — correct answer conspicuously longer than distractors) but does not trip the hard aggregate rate threshold.

per_question.note: Single-word options; absolute delta is 2-4 chars; ratio 1.304 flags it but is a measurement artefact of the very short stripped option labels.

per_question.correct_text_stripped: Creating a Python dictionary with one key per role, then assembling with "\n\n".join() in priority order
Constraint wins because the priority rule states Constraint overrides Exemplar when they conflict
The output structure will vary from request to request — sometimes numbered steps, sometimes paragraphs — because the model picks its own format without a Rubric
per_question.note: Soft flag: ratio 1.857 is the highest in the set. Correct answer carries a concrete scenario clause that distractors omit. Does not breach the 0.40 aggregate rate threshold.

scoring_notes.q1: Constraint(3), Rubric(3), Metadata(2) — all plausible role-confusion errors; none absurd.
scoring_notes.q2: Authority(4) and Exemplar(4) are very strong distractors given the adjacent prompt context; Rubric(3) plausible.
scoring_notes.q3: Concatenation-with-+(4), system=-inline(3), text-file(3) are all natural Python patterns a beginner would reach for.
scoring_notes.q4: Exemplar-wins(3) exploits in-context-learning intuition; Rubric-wins(2) and Metadata-wins(2) weaker but grounded in corpus vocabulary.
scoring_notes.q5: All three distractors map to other role absences described in corpus §5 — plausible role-confusion errors; scores 3/3/3.

beginner_fairness_and_scope.notes: All stems use only vocabulary defined in topic_corpus.md. Every question is answerable by a student who has read this topic and nothing beyond weeks 13.1-13.6. No advanced/adjacent concepts (RAG, agents, fine-tuning) appear in stems or distractors.

soft_flags.detail: q5 correct-answer ratio is 1.857 after feedback stripping — noticeably longer than distractors. Aggregate rate stays at 0.40 (boundary, not a hard fail). If a further revision pass occurs, trimming the correct answer or expanding distractor elaboration is recommended.

verdict: Pass — all five checks clean (second attempt, post length-bias revision). Positional bias max_share=0.40 and length-bias rate=0.40 both sit exactly at the 0.40 boundary (not strictly greater, per SKILL threshold >0.40). q5 carries a soft length-ratio flag (1.857) that does not trip the hard fail. Distractor plausibility absurd_rate=0.0, all 5 LOs covered, no orphan questions, no out-of-scope stems.
